description:
Goal
Build the authoritative audit of remaining compiler2 `any`/`none` producers after ReturnEvidence lands, and pin each category with an intent test before changing behavior.

Signal
- A grep/audit table classifies every `any_ty(world)` and `none_ty(world)` in semantic/type flow as explicit dynamic top, explicit empty/no-return bottom, impossible input, or bug-to-fix in a child ticket.
- Tests in `_test.rs` files capture the intended behavior for missing return evidence, unresolved module/protocol state, dynamic provider boundaries, impossible input evidence, and true no-return.
- The audit does not bless a fallback merely because current tests pass; disputed sites become blockers in this chain.
- The audit commit contains no long-lived shim or adapter API.

Tactics
- Start from `rg -n "any_ty\(|none_ty\(" src/compiler2 -g '*.rs'` and trace each result to the earliest fact/evidence site.
- Use telemetry where possible to prove whether a top/bottom value freezes a frontier or is just a real domain boundary.
- Keep the table close to agent docs only if it will remain useful; otherwise let the tests be the durable record.

Outro
No unclassified semantic top/bottom producer, no comment-only TODO in place of a ticket, no test that accepts `any`/`none` because it is convenient, and no new helper that hides the existing constructors under friendlier names.
title: Compiler2 type convergence: audit semantic top/bottom producers
